summary: Package fetches and executes payload from anonymous Cloudflare R2 bucket
details: dist/bootstrap.js fetches content from https://pub-046c52795b9445cd9f5cc5cb21b9d59f.r2.dev — an anonymous Cloudflare R2 bucket whose content is mutable and not bound to any verifiable publisher identity. The bootstrap file makes multiple fetch() calls and references process.env, indicating environment-variable handling alongside the remote fetch. R2-hosted payloads from pub-*.r2.dev have been observed as the second-stage drop infrastructure in active npm dropper clusters; the host shape itself (anonymous bucket, no version pinning, no hash verification, no relation to any documented publisher) is the malicious-dropper fingerprint regardless of the cover story. Installing this package and triggering the bootstrap path causes the installer to execute arbitrary attacker-controlled bytes that can be rotated at any time.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
